question_text: An aluminium block has a density of 2.7 g/cm³ and a volume of 100 cm³. Calculate its mass. Then compare its density to that of water (1 g/cm³) and explain what this tells you about how aluminium behaves in water.
correct_answer: 270 g. Aluminium is 2.7 times denser than water, so a solid block of aluminium will sink in water
explanation: Mass = Density × Volume = 2.7 g/cm³ × 100 cm³ = 270 g. Since aluminium's density (2.7 g/cm³) is greater than water's (1 g/cm³), aluminium is denser and sinks. Objects denser than a fluid sink in that fluid.
